sentences.text: Quality of Service (QoS) is defined as the minimal end-to-end performance guaranteed in advance by a service level agreement (SLA) to a workload.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Performance metrics might include measures such as instructions per cycle (IPC), latency of service, etc.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: When multiple workloads execute concurrently on modern processors—equipped with large core counts, multiple cache hierarchies, and multiple memory controllers— the performance of any given workload becomes less deterministic, or even non-deterministic, due to shared resource contention.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: To manage performance variability, system software needs resource allocation and monitoring capabilities.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: These capabilities allow for the reservation of resources like cache and bandwidth, thus meeting individual performance targets while minimizing interference.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For resource management, hardware should provide monitoring features that allow system software to profile workload resource consumption and allocate resources accordingly.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: To facilitate this, the QoS Identifiers extension (Ssqosid) introduces the `srmcfg` register, which configures a hart with two identifiers: a Resource Control ID (`RCID`) and a Monitoring Counter ID (`MCID`).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: These identifiers accompany each request issued by the hart to shared resource controllers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Additional metadata, like the nature of the memory access and the ID of the originating supervisor domain, can accompany `RCID` and `MCID`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Resource controllers may use this metadata for differentiated service such as a different capacity allocation for code storage vs. data storage.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Resource controllers can use this data for security policies such as not exposing statistics of one security domain to another.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: These identifiers are crucial for the RISC-V Capacity and Bandwidth Controller QoS Register Interface (CBQRI) specification, which provides methods for setting resource usage limits and monitoring resource consumption.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The `RCID` controls resource allocations, while the `MCID` is used for tracking resource usage.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: NOTE: The Ssqosid extension does not require that S-mode mode be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The `srmcfg` register is an SXLEN-bit read/write register used to configure a Resource Control ID (`RCID`) and a Monitoring Counter ID (`MCID`).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: Both `RCID` and `MCID` are WARL fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: The register is formatted as shown in when SXLEN=64 and when SXLEN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: The `RCID` and `MCID` accompany each request made by the hart to shared resource controllers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: The `RCID` is used to determine the resource allocations (e.g., cache occupancy limits, memory bandwidth limits, etc.) to enforce.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: The `MCID` is used to identify a counter to monitor resource usage.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: .Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register for SXLEN=64
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , ] .... {reg: [ {bits: 12, name: 'RCID'}, {bits: 4, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 12, name: 'MCID'}, {bits: 36, name: 'WPRI'}, ], config:{lanes: 2, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: .Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register for SXLEN=32
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , ] .... {reg: [ {bits: 12, name: 'RCID'}, {bits: 4, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 12, name: 'MCID'}, {bits: 4, name: 'WPRI'}, ], config:{lanes: 1, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: The `RCID` and `MCID` configured in the `srmcfg` CSR apply to all privilege modes of software execution on that hart by default, but this behavior may be overridden by future extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: If extension Smstateen is implemented together with Ssqosid, then Ssqosid also requires the SRMCFG bit in `mstateen0` to be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: If `mstateen0`.SRMCFG is 0, attempts to access `srmcfg` in privilege modes less privileged than M-mode raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register

sentences.text: If `mstateen0`.SRMCFG is 1 or if extension Smstateen is not implemented, attempts to access `srmcfg` when `V=1` raise a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Ssqosid" Extension for Quality-of-Service (QoS) Identifiers, Version 1.0 > Supervisor Resource Management Configuration (`srmcfg`) register
